plant.description: Unique History: Classified under the 'Artavasangrahaniya' (menstrual-regulating) and 'Sandhaniya' (healing) groups in ancient Ayurvedic treatises like Charaka Samhita. The name 'Lodhra' signifies its association with Lord Shiva. It is spiritually associated with 'Sattva' (purity and clarity). Mentioned in classical Sanskrit literature as the 'Lodh Tree'. Physical Characteristics: A medium-sized evergreen tree reaching up to 60 meters (rarely) but usually 10-15 meters in height, with a majestic crown and brownish-grey, rough bark. The leaves are alternate, ovate-elliptic, and leathery. The flowers are attractive, white-to-yellowish, fragrant, in terminal or axillary racemes. The fruit is an oblong drupe. It is found throughout India, particularly in the sub-Himalayan tract and Northeast forests. Specific Chemical Compounds: Primarily contains Tannins (approx. 10-15% in bark), Alkaloids (Loturine), and Saponins. The presence of loturine is crucial for its neuro-relaxant, anti-inflammatory, and anti-tubercular effects (when Appropriately diluted). Strict Standardizations: The stem bark is standardized in API based on its brownish-grey color and characteristic astringent-slimy-bitter taste. Physicochemical benchmarks: total ash (<10%), alcohol-soluble extractive (>5%), and water-soluble extractive (>15%). HPLC is used to monitor 5% to 10% tannins in commercial extracts.

ayurvedic_properties.dosha_karma: Powerful Pitta and Kapha Hara (pacifies Pitta and Kapha doshas). Due to its Kashaya (astringent) Rasa and Sheeta (cooling) Virya, it is the most prestigious herb for 'Artavasangrahaniya' (menstrual-regulating) and 'Shothahara' (anti-inflammatory) therapies, clearing the fiery and agitated qualities of Pitta while balancing the heavy, cold, and sticky qualities of Kapha through its systemic astringent property. It does not aggravate Vata when used appropriately.

stem_bark_powder__churna_.method: 3-6g of fine powder taken twice daily with milk or honey (for female health) or warm water (for general vitality).

bark_decoction__kwatha_.method: 20g of bark boiled in 160ml water reduced to 40ml for chronic uterine inflammation and diarrhea. Take twice daily before meals.

cold_infusion__hima_.method: 10g of crushed bark soaked in 60ml water overnight; filtered and taken with sugar in the morning for cooling and acidity.

lodhrasava.method: 15-30ml of fermented extract taken twice daily after meals for managing menstrual issues and general weakness.

external_paste__lepa_.method: Ground fresh bark or powder applied directly to localized inflammations (Mumps, boils, skin rashes) to reduce heat and swelling.

dosage.children: Generally safe. 125 mg to 250 mg of powder mixed with honey once or twice daily for digestive protection and skin issues during childhood infections.
dosage.adults: Powder: 3-6 grams; Decoction: 20-50 ml; Arishta: 15-30 ml; divided throughout the day before meals.
dosage.elderly: Ideal for age-related Pitta issues and maintaining cardiac health. Dosage: 3g of powder daily with warm milk at bedtime.

medicinal_uses: World-Famous Female Reproductive Specialist: Lodhra is the premier Ayurvedic remedy for all conditions of excessive menstrual bleeding (Asrigdara) and vaginal secretions (Pradara). Its 'Artavasangrahaniya' (menstrual-regulating) and 'Kashaya' (astringent) nature provided by tannins and alkaloids helps in providing systemic hormonal balance, reducing uterine inflammation, and providing hemostatic control. WHO-aligned research confirms its role in managing inflammatory gynecological load.

Skin Health and Acne Management: Known as 'Varnya' (beneficial for skin). Its cooling and blood-purifying properties help in treating chronic skin conditions like acne (Yuvana-pidika), psoriasis, and eczema. The bark paste applied topically promotes rapid relief from acne and sun-burns while providing systemic 'Raktaprasadana' (blood-soothing) effects.

Vision and Eye health Support: Acts as a powerfulagent for managing eye health. It helps in reducing redness, burning sensations, and discharge from the eyes in conditions like conjunctivitis (Abhishyanda). It is traditionally used in 'Akshi-roga' (eye-disease) management through its systemic cooling action.

Gastrointestinal and Diarrhea Care: Its 'Grahi' (absorbent) and 'Kashaya' (astringent) actions make it highly effective for managing burning hemorrhoids, chronic diarrhea, and inflammatory bowel conditions by strengthening the gut lining and binding the stool.

Wound Healing and Hemostatic Control: Traditionally used to manage internal and external bleeding (Raktapitta) and non-healing ulcers. Its cooling and astringent properties help in providing systemic hemostatic balance and promoting healthy skin recovery through its 'Sandhaniya' (healing) quality.

Dento-Gingival and Oral care: Its astringent nature makes it an excellent antimicrobial agent for oral infections and bleeding gums. The bark powder is traditionally used in 'Danta-dhavana' (tooth-cleaning) to strengthen the gums and prevent inflammation.

Phytochemical Profile: Rich in Tannins, Triterpenoids (Betulin, Lupeol), Alkaloids (Loturine, Colloturine), and Flavonoids. Loturine and Tannins are the primary bioactives responsible for its potent anti-inflammatory, anti-microbial, and tissue-contracting action.

safety_profile.warning: GRAS Status: Classified as a safe medicinal herb with a history of use spanning millennia across various global cultures and traditional systems (Ayurveda, Unani, and TCM) without documented toxicity.
safety_profile.medical_supervision: Use under the guidance of a qualified Ayurvedic practitioner.
contraindications: Generally very safe. However, individuals with high Kapha (cold, excess mucus, obesity) should use it in moderation as it is Kashaya (astringent). Avoid during active extreme constipation due to its 'Grahi' (absorbent) nature.
possible_side_effects: None documented at traditional therapeutic levels. Excessive use in very sensitive individuals may rarely cause mild skin cooling or stomach dryness.
drug_interactions: Monitor if taken alongside synthetic anti-diuretic or anticoagulant medications as it may potentiate their effects. No severe adverse interactions reported in clinical literature.

plant_profile.note: AI estimated profile based on known properties
